module_mood_contract: permission beat: you will want multi-session; find your way over time, no rush — earned competence without pressure

rules_evaluated.note: Reference page, not a lecture. No placement decision required.

rules_evaluated.rule: 1 — mood contract named; mood-floor 8+/10 per teaching beat
rules_evaluated.note: Mood contract (permission beat) is explicit at lines 5 and 15. All 8 phases score 8–9/10. No teaching beat below 8. No setup-beat carve-outs needed — every section teaches something.
'Don't tie your brain in knots. A thirty-second fix.' (line 68). Conflict recovery section (lines 74–76) is firm without blame. Consistently epistemic throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule: 3 — lead with discipline not failure mode
rules_evaluated.note: All sections lead positively: 'Git is what makes this safe,' 'Worktrees are the middle path,' 'The permission beat, once.' Failure modes appear as named callouts within sections, not as framing leads.

File is exactly what AE101 content strategy §M1 homework #2 prescribes: worktrees vs branches vs clones, N-session workflow, stashing, conflict recovery, wrong-branch recovery, permission beat. No strategy-sequence violations detected.

rules_evaluated.rule: 5 — strategic themes recur in Key Concepts
rules_evaluated.note: Reference page. No Key Concepts section. Theme resonance is present in body (compounding, non-agentic default, capture-your-world) but Key Concepts format N/A.
No Key Concepts section. Reference page format.

rules_evaluated.rule: 27 — body teaches the work; no authored conversation cues
rules_evaluated.note: Body is purely technical prose about Git mechanics and session workflow. No pause cues, no 'discuss with,' no trainer-bait sentences.

rules_evaluated.rule: 16 — forcing functions live in prompts not body prose
rules_evaluated.note: No fenced prompt blocks in this reference page. Rule does not apply.
'You do not have to start here' (line 4), 'Find your way at your pace' (line 90), 'When NOT to run multi-session' names conditions without prescribing — respects adult autonomy throughout.
No fenced prompt blocks in this reference page. Prompt rules do not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule: M1→M2 bridge: closes with permission-beat tone, sets up M2 plan-mode work
rules_evaluated.note: Close section (lines 89–90): 'Two sessions is a milestone. Five is a habit. Ten is something you grow into. Find your way at your pace.' Unlocks multi-session without front-running M2's plan-mode-at-depth. Student arrives at M2 with worktree-readiness + zero pressure to parallelise before they're ready.
